_note: Manual in-game crafting difficulty-color observations, used to reverse-engineer recipe trivials the wiki is missing (it shows '?'). Difficulty colors easiest->hardest: green (= trivial, no more skill-ups), lightblue, darkblue, white (~50% success), yellow, orange, red. A recipe seen GREEN at skill S has trivial <= S; any other color means trivial > S. Recording the same recipe at several skill levels pins the exact trivial (it turns green at its trivial) and calibrates each colour's skill-gap. Edit this file as you hit new skill milestones; the site merges it over the wiki recipe data.

_model.note: Trivials land on MULTIPLES OF 5 (in-game breakpoints are every 5 levels), and each colour band is 5 skill points wide. This single model reproduces the skill-48, 50 AND 55 readings exactly for every recipe (3 milestones, 0 mismatches), so trivial estimates below are confident to the nearest 5. Red items remain a lower bound until they shift colour.

Blacksmithing.note: All copper recipes not listed here were green/trivial at skill 48. Enchanted variants matched their base item's colour.
greenTrivialAtOrBelow: (and every other copper recipe not listed above)

Blacksmithing.note: +2 skill from the level-48 reading. Confirms the multiples-of-5 / 5-wide-band model: the level-48 lightblue recipes are now exactly green (trivial 50). Enchanted variants match their base item's colour.
greenTrivialAtOrBelow: (and every other copper recipe not listed above)

Blacksmithing.note: Third reading, fits the model exactly again. Copper War Lance turned GREEN at exactly 55 (confirms trivial 55). The copper weapons read yellow (= 75, confirming the earlier orange@50). The whole bronze tier + jagged stones read orange (= 80). Silver reads RED, one band HARDER than the orange bronze, so Silver = ~85 — note the wiki lists Silver Chain Links/Plate at 80, which this contradicts. Bronze Chain Gorget wasn't visible in this reading.
greenTrivialAtOrBelow: (and every other copper recipe not listed above)

trivialEstimates._method: Derived from the colour at skill 48, 50 AND 55 via the multiples-of-5 / 5-wide-band model in _model, which reproduces all three readings exactly. Values are the nearest multiple of 5. Confirmed by the 55 reading: Copper War Lance = 55 (turned green), the copper weapons = 75 (yellow), the bronze tier + jagged stones = 80 (orange). Silver read RED at 55 (one band above bronze) => Silver ~85, which CONTRADICTS the wiki's listed 80 for Silver Chain Links/Plate; trust this once silver turns green ~85. Bronze Chain Gorget not yet re-observed (still a lower bound). Enchanted variants share their base item's trivial.
